targetApp: xx memory (通用智能记忆助手) Android App
purpose: Enable any AI agent to generate flashcard files in formats the xx memory app can import. The AI reads this skill definition, then produces ready-to-import files (JSON, CSV, TXT, or Markdown) containing well-structured memory cards following the app's schema and quality standards.
whenToUse: A user asks the AI to create flashcards / memory cards / 记忆卡片 for the xx memory app.
A user asks to convert their notes, textbook content, code snippets, or study material into importable flashcard files.
A user explicitly mentions any of the supported import formats (JSON / CSV / TXT / Markdown) for xx memory.
A user references 'xx memory', '通用智能记忆助手', or asks for cards compatible with this app's import flow.

qa.name: 问答 (Question & Answer)
qa.description: Classic front/back card. Use question as the prompt and answer as the expected response.

example.question: What is the capital of France?

fill_blank.name: 填空 (Fill in the Blank)
fill_blank.description: A cloze-style card. Put the blank token (e.g. ___) inside the question and supply the missing text in answer.

example.question: The powerhouse of the cell is the ___.

code.name: 代码 (Code)
code.description: Programming card. Place the code problem / prompt in question and the code (optionally with explanation) in answer. Preserve indentation and newlines exactly.

image.name: 图片 (Image)
image.description: Visual recall card. imageUrl is required and must be a valid URL or local path resolvable by the app. question is the caption / prompt, answer is the expected recall target.

audio.name: 音频 (Audio)
audio.description: Audio recall card. audioUrl is required. question is the prompt, answer is what the learner should recognize or recall after listening.

question.description: The front of the card / the prompt shown to the learner.

answer.description: The back of the card / the expected response.

subject.description: Category / subject grouping (e.g. Biology, JavaScript, Spanish). Cards with the same subject are grouped together in the app.

detail.description: Optional extra explanation shown after the answer is revealed. Use for context, mnemonics, or source references.

cardType.description: Determines how the card is rendered and reviewed. Must be one of the supported card types.

tags.description: Comma-separated tag list (e.g. 'algebra,exam,chapter3'). Used for filtering and search.

imageUrl.description: URL or local path to an image. Required when cardType=image, otherwise empty.

audioUrl.description: URL or local path to an audio file. Required when cardType=audio, otherwise empty.

isFavorite.description: Whether the card is marked as favorite / starred in the app.

json.description: A JSON array of card objects. Recommended format — lossless and easiest to validate.
rules: Root element MUST be a JSON array, even for a single card.
Every object MUST contain at minimum: question, answer, cardType.
Use UTF-8 encoding. Preserve all newlines in code answers with \n.
Booleans (isFavorite) MUST be JSON true/false, not strings.
Omit or leave empty string for optional fields you do not use; do not use null.
Do not include comments, trailing commas, or BOM.

example.question: What is the time complexity of binary search?
example.subject: Algorithms
example.detail: Halves the search space each step.

example.question: Complete: The keyword to define a constant in JavaScript is ___.
example.subject: JavaScript

example.question: Write a function to check if a number is even in Python.
example.subject: Python

csv.description: Comma-separated values with a header row. Useful for spreadsheet workflows.
rules: First line MUST be the header row with these exact columns in order: question,answer,subject,detail,cardType,tags,imageUrl,audioUrl,isFavorite
Every subsequent line is one card.
Fields containing commas, double quotes, or newlines MUST be wrapped in double quotes.
Inside quoted fields, escape double quotes by doubling them ("").
Newlines inside a field (e.g. code answers) MUST be wrapped in double quotes.
isFavorite values must be true or false (lowercase).
Use UTF-8 encoding.
csv.example:
question,answer,subject,detail,cardType,tags,imageUrl,audioUrl,isFavorite
"What is the time complexity of binary search?","O(log n)",Algorithms,"Halves the search space each step.",qa,"complexity,search,basics",,,false
"Complete: The keyword to define a constant in JavaScript is ___.",const,JavaScript,,fill_blank,syntax,es6,,true
"Write a function to check if a number is even in Python.","def is_even(n):\n    return n % 2 == 0",Python,"Uses the modulo operator.",code,"functions,basics",,,false
"What organelle is shown?",Mitochondria,Biology,,image,"cell,organelle",https://example.com/mito.png,,false
"Which instrument plays this melody?",Violin,Music,,audio,strings,listening,,https://example.com/violin.mp3,false

txt.description: Plain-text marker format. One card per block, blocks separated by a blank line. Human-friendly and diff-friendly.
rules: Each card is a block of lines; separate cards with a single blank line.
The block MUST start with a Q: line containing the question.
The block MUST contain an A: line containing the answer.
Optional metadata lines (any order, after Q:/A:) begin with one of: Subject:, Detail:, CardType:, Tags:, ImageUrl:, AudioUrl:, Favorite:.
CardType defaults to qa if omitted. Allowed values: qa, fill_blank, code, image, audio.
Favorite: line accepts true or false (case-insensitive); defaults to false.
Tags: is a comma-separated list on a single line.
For multi-line code answers, indent continuation lines with 4 spaces, or wrap the entire answer following A: on subsequent indented lines.
Use UTF-8 encoding.
txt.example:
Q: What is the time complexity of binary search?
A: O(log n)
Subject: Algorithms
Detail: Halves the search space each step.
CardType: qa
Tags: complexity,search,basics
Favorite: false

Q: Complete: The keyword to define a constant in JavaScript is ___.
A: const
Subject: JavaScript
CardType: fill_blank
Tags: syntax,es6
Favorite: true

Q: Write a function to check if a number is even in Python.
A: def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 == 0
Subject: Python
Detail: Uses the modulo operator.
CardType: code
Tags: functions,basics

Q: What organelle is shown?
A: Mitochondria
Subject: Biology
CardType: image
Tags: cell,organelle
ImageUrl: https://example.com/mito.png

Q: Which instrument plays this melody?
A: Violin
Subject: Music
CardType: audio
Tags: strings,listening
AudioUrl: https://example.com/violin.mp3

markdown.description: Markdown format using headings to delimit cards and metadata lines for app-specific fields.
rules: Each card is a level-2 heading section starting with '## ' followed by the question text.
Immediately under the heading, the answer MUST appear as a paragraph prefixed with '**Answer:** ' (or a line starting with 'Answer: ').
Optional detail follows as a paragraph prefixed with '**Detail:** '.
App metadata uses heading-comments / list lines beginning with '# Tags:', '# CardType:', '# Subject:', '# ImageUrl:', '# AudioUrl:', '# Favorite:'.
CardType defaults to qa. Allowed: qa, fill_blank, code, image, audio.
Favorite accepts true / false; defaults to false.
Code answers SHOULD use a fenced code block (```), preserving indentation.
Separate cards with a horizontal rule (---) or a blank line plus the next '## ' heading.
Use UTF-8 encoding.
markdown.example:
## What is the time complexity of binary search?

**Answer:** O(log n)

**Detail:** Halves the search space each step.

# Subject: Algorithms
# CardType: qa
# Tags: complexity,search,basics
# Favorite: false

---

## Complete: The keyword to define a constant in JavaScript is ___.

**Answer:** const

# Subject: JavaScript
# CardType: fill_blank
# Tags: syntax,es6
# Favorite: true

---

## Write a function to check if a number is even in Python.

**Answer:**

```python
def is_even(n):
    return n % 2 == 0
```

**Detail:** Uses the modulo operator.

# Subject: Python
# CardType: code
# Tags: functions,basics

---

## What organelle is shown?

**Answer:** Mitochondria

![card image](https://example.com/mito.png)

# Subject: Biology
# CardType: image
# Tags: cell,organelle
# ImageUrl: https://example.com/mito.png

---

## Which instrument plays this melody?

**Answer:** Violin

# Subject: Music
# CardType: audio
# Tags: strings,listening
# AudioUrl: https://example.com/violin.mp3

qualityGuidelines.atomicity: One concept per card. If a question needs two answers, split it into two cards.
qualityGuidelines.conciseness: Keep questions and answers short. Aim for answers that fit on one line where possible; move lengthy context into the detail field.
qualityGuidelines.clarity: Use plain, unambiguous language. Avoid pronouns whose referent is unclear without surrounding context.
qualityGuidelines.selfContained: Each card must be understandable in isolation, without relying on previous cards.
qualityGuidelines.answerSpecificity: Answers should be specific and verifiable. Avoid 'it depends' style answers; if nuance is needed, put it in detail.
qualityGuidelines.codeCards: Code must be syntactically valid for the stated language and preserve exact indentation/newlines. Include only the minimal code that answers the question.
qualityGuidelines.tags: Use 2–5 meaningful tags per card. Prefer lowercase, no spaces inside a tag (use hyphens if needed).
qualityGuidelines.subject: Reuse consistent subject names across cards so they group cleanly in the app.
qualityGuidelines.noDuplicates: Do not emit duplicate cards. Merge or discard near-duplicates before writing the file.
qualityGuidelines.avoidTriviaOnly: Favor understanding and recall over rote trivia; prefer questions that probe concepts over isolated facts when possible.

generationProcedure: Identify the source material (notes, textbook, code, audio/image list) the user provides.
Choose the target output format. If unspecified, default to JSON.
Break the material into atomic concepts and decide a cardType for each.
Draft question + answer pairs; put supporting context into detail, not into the question.
Assign a consistent subject and 2–5 tags per card.
If cardType is image/audio, ensure imageUrl/audioUrl is present and resolvable.
Render the cards in the chosen format, following its rules exactly (see outputFormats).
Validate: every card has question, answer, cardType; every image card has imageUrl; every audio card has audioUrl; no duplicate Q/A pairs.
Return the file content to the user (and, if writing to disk, save with the appropriate extension: .json / .csv / .txt / .md).
validationChecklist: All required fields present (question, answer, cardType) on every card.
cardType is one of: qa, fill_blank, code, image, audio.
image cards have a non-empty imageUrl; audio cards have a non-empty audioUrl.
No duplicate questions within the file.
Tags are comma-separated with no spaces inside individual tags (spaces around commas are tolerated).
JSON: array root, valid JSON, no trailing commas.
CSV: header row exact, quoted fields where needed, newlines within quotes.
TXT: Q:/A: present, blank line between cards.
Markdown: '## ' heading per card, Answer line present, metadata lines prefixed with '# '.
notes: This skill is format-only. It does not interact with the xx memory app's database or scheduling engine; the app imports these files through its own import flow.
When in doubt about field semantics, prefer the JSON format — it is lossless and unambiguous.
If the user gives existing flashcards in one format and asks to convert to another, parse using the source format's rules then re-render using the target format's rules; do not change card content.
